ocean_info: Aruba sits in the southern Caribbean Sea, roughly 15 miles off the coast of Venezuela. The island lies outside the Atlantic hurricane belt and enjoys dry, sunny trade-wind weather most of the year. For first-time tourists, the leeward west and south coasts feel like a classic Caribbean postcard: calm turquoise water, fine white sand, and gentle waves ideal for swimming and sunbathing. Snorkelers and divers should know that Aruba has two very different coastlines. The west and northwest shores (Eagle Beach, Palm Beach, Malmok, Boca Catalina, Arashi, Mangel Halto) offer protected water with reef patches, sea turtles, and colorful fish close to shore. The east and north shores face the open Atlantic — dramatic cliffs, powerful surf, and strong currents; beautiful for photos and experienced surfers, but generally not for casual swimming. Water temperature stays warm year-round (about 26–29°C / 79–84°F). Visibility is often excellent on calm mornings. All beaches in Aruba are public by law. Use reef-safe sunscreen and never take sand, coral, or shells.

coast_guide.west_south: Calm Caribbean side — swimming, snorkeling, resorts, families
coast_guide.east_north: Wild Atlantic side — scenery, surfing, photography; swimming usually not safe

characteristics.depth_profile: Shallow sandy entry for roughly 50–100 cm over white sand; reef and rocky patches begin within 20–40 m of shore on the left (south) side when facing the sea. Depth increases gradually to 2–4 m over reef, then drops more quickly beyond the fringing reef edge.
characteristics.stone_rocks: Moderate and sporadic along the shoreline — mixed sand and low limestone rock. Rocky patches near the reef hold the best fish life. There are open sandy lanes with fewer rocks, ideal for easy entry.
characteristics.crowds: moderate; quieter on weekdays and north of the main parking area
characteristics.access: Free parking lot; Bus L10 from Oranjestad; ~15–20 min drive north from Palm Beach past California Lighthouse
characteristics.facilities: Free palapas (arrive early), snack hut, occasional chair rentals
fun_facts: Name may derive from Papiamento 'warashi' (bonefish) — historically important in the indigenous Caquetio diet.
Part of the island's 'Gold Coast' nickname for spectacular sunsets facing west over the Caribbean.

We saw a family feeding fish in the middle of a small patch, attracting a huge concentration of different species. Suddenly pelicans would sweep in and grab one or two as dinner. Having snorkel goggles underwater where a pelican might land is definitely something not to miss — but you have to time it.
Blue parrotfish, French angelfish, and green sea turtles are regular sightings on the reef to the left of the main beach.
safety_notes: Best snorkeling on calm mornings; currents can strengthen on windy days near the reef edge.

characteristics.depth_profile: Very gradual sandy slope — waist-deep for a long distance on calm days. No significant reef at this beach; depth stays shallow (under 1.5 m) for tens of meters before slow deepening.
characteristics.stone_rocks: Minimal — predominantly fine white sand; occasional small shells
characteristics.crowds: moderate; busier near Palm Beach end, quieter toward Manchebo
characteristics.access: Free parking lots along the coast road; taxi and bus accessible
characteristics.facilities: Palapas, nearby beach bars and restaurants at adjacent resorts (public beach access)
fun_facts: Repeatedly ranked among the best beaches in the Caribbean and top beaches worldwide on TripAdvisor.
Iconic wind-bent fofoti (divi-divi) trees line the shore — one of Aruba's most photographed scenes.
Important sea turtle nesting site (roughly May–November); nests may be marked with tape — do not disturb.

characteristics.depth_profile: Gentle sandy slope; shallow and safe for long distances. Reef snorkeling is limited from shore — most snorkel tours depart from here by boat.
characteristics.stone_rocks: Minimal on main swim areas — wide groomed sand
characteristics.crowds: busy, especially high season and near high-rise hotels
characteristics.access: Paid lots near beach center; free side-street parking a short walk away
characteristics.facilities: Extensive — restaurants, bars, jet skis, parasailing, paddleboards, tour operators
fun_facts: Two-mile resort strip backed by high-rise hotels — Aruba's main tourist hub.
Best launch point for sunset cruises, catamaran snorkel trips, and Antilla shipwreck tours.

characteristics.depth_profile: Very gradual; among the widest beach sections on Aruba — shallow water extends far from shore.
characteristics.stone_rocks: Minimal — open powdery sand
characteristics.access: Free parking; continuous with Eagle Beach to the north
characteristics.facilities: Low-rise resort palapas nearby; fewer commercial vendors than Palm Beach
fun_facts: Effectively the southern continuation of Eagle Beach with a more peaceful, adults-oriented resort feel.

characteristics.depth_profile: Shallow sandy entry similar to Eagle Beach; gradual slope.
characteristics.stone_rocks: Low rock promontories at edges add texture but main swim zone is sandy
characteristics.access: Free parking; closer to Oranjestad and cruise terminal than Palm Beach
characteristics.facilities: Beach Bar Aruba on the sand; some palapas
fun_facts: Walk south to reach Eagle Beach easily — popular sunset happy hour spot among locals and repeat visitors.

characteristics.depth_profile: Enclosed lagoon: ankle to waist-deep for a very long distance inside the natural reef breakwater. Depth increases near the left-side rock barrier where reef fish gather; do not swim beyond protected areas — open ocean side can be dangerous.
characteristics.stone_rocks: Rock barrier defines the lagoon; sandy bottom inside; reef and rocks at the channel edges
characteristics.crowds: moderate to busy on weekends; arrive early
characteristics.access: Free large parking lot; ~45 min drive from Palm Beach through San Nicolas
characteristics.facilities: Two beach restaurants, restrooms, snorkel gear rentals
fun_facts: Named for pool-like calm water — toddlers can splash safely in the lagoon interior.
Snorkeling improves near the left rock barrier: parrotfish, trunkfish, and sea turtles.
Drive passes San Nicolas street murals — worth combining with a south-coast culture stop.
safety_notes: Stay inside the protected lagoon; outer reef openings can have strong current and surge.

characteristics.depth_profile: Sandy slope with moderate depth increase; less enclosed than Baby Beach.
characteristics.stone_rocks: Some rocky sections; mostly sand
characteristics.access: Free parking near Baby Beach area; short drive from San Nicolas
characteristics.facilities: Very limited — bring water and snacks
fun_facts: Quieter neighbor to Baby Beach near the fishing village of Savaneta — more local, less touristy feel.

characteristics.depth_profile: Rocky and mangrove-fringed entry — no long sand beach. Shallow patch reef within meters of shore, then reef drops to roughly 30 m (100 ft) in places. Channels between mangrove roots hold juvenile fish.
characteristics.stone_rocks: Extensive — rocky shore, wooden staircases into water; water shoes recommended
characteristics.sand: Minimal — mangrove and limestone coastline rather than classic sand beach
characteristics.access: Free roadside parking; near Savaneta
characteristics.facilities: Minimal — benches, wooden stairs; no rentals on site
fun_facts: Often rated the #1 shore snorkeling spot in Aruba for reef diversity.
Mangroves act as fish nurseries — French angelfish, parrotfish, blue tang, eels, and turtles.
Solar-powered sea scooter tours popular when current is stronger.
safety_notes: Current varies — visit at low tide for best visibility; use guided tour if inexperienced with drift.

characteristics.depth_profile: Protected small cove — shallow reef within 10–30 m of shore; gradual depth to 2–3 m over coral heads.
characteristics.stone_rocks: Rocky in places around cove edges; sandy pocket in center
characteristics.access: Small roadside parking between Malmok and Arashi; arrive early
characteristics.facilities: None — bring own snorkel gear, water, and shade
fun_facts: Small protected cove locals prefer to keep low-key — catamarans pass offshore while you snorkel.
Marine reserve protections help fish populations thrive close to shore.

characteristics.depth_profile: Concrete steps into deep water quickly — turtles often rest on sandy bottom just beyond roped swim area.
characteristics.stone_rocks: Rocky point — not a sand beach; steps carved into limestone
characteristics.access: Roadside parking on northwest coast near Malmok
fun_facts: One of the most reliable places to snorkel with sea turtles from shore.
Bright blue whiptail lizards and pelicans on the rocks add to the scene.
Often combined with Boca Catalina and Arashi for a north-coast snorkel morning.
safety_notes: Watch current; rash guard recommended; stay within marked swim zones.

characteristics.depth_profile: Rocky shoreline with reef patches close in; depth increases over coral within 20–50 m.
characteristics.stone_rocks: Moderate to extensive — rocky coast with sandy patches
characteristics.access: Free roadside parking between Palm Beach and Boca Catalina
fun_facts: Quieter than resort beaches; good coral formations; occasional sea glass found by beachcombers.

characteristics.depth_profile: Shallow flat water ideal for board sports — sandy bottom extends far at low wind chop.
characteristics.stone_rocks: Minimal in launch zone
characteristics.access: Along coast road north of Palm Beach; near Aruban wind sports schools
fun_facts: Steady trade winds make this Aruba's premier windsurfing and kitesurfing beach.

characteristics.depth_profile: Shallow sandy slope near Oranjestad waterfront.
characteristics.stone_rocks: Minimal
characteristics.access: ~15 minute walk from Aruba cruise terminal; taxi from Oranjestad
fun_facts: Most convenient beach for cruise passengers with limited port time.
Plane spotting possible — aircraft approach nearby.

characteristics.depth_profile: Calm protected bay on private island — shallow sandy bottom.
characteristics.stone_rocks: Minimal
characteristics.access: Boat from Renaissance Wind Creek Resort; day pass for non-guests
characteristics.facilities: Full service — bar, restaurant, chairs included with access
fun_facts: Pink flamingos roam freely among sunbathers — one of the Caribbean's most iconic photo spots.
Not a public beach — requires hotel stay or purchased day pass.

characteristics.depth_profile: Shallow turbulent zone near shore with powerful dumpers; rapid offshore depth but unsafe for swimmers.
characteristics.stone_rocks: Extensive — craggy cliffs and rock formations
characteristics.access: 4x4 recommended; east coast road
fun_facts: Dramatic Atlantic landscape — roar of surf and cliff scenery; favorite of bodyboarders.
safety_notes: Do not swim — strong currents and powerful shore break.

characteristics.depth_profile: Two connected wild coves with surf zone only — no safe wading depth for casual swimmers.
characteristics.stone_rocks: Rocky coves within Arikok National Park
characteristics.access: 4x4 or hike; Arikok National Park entrance fee
fun_facts: 'Two beaches' inside Arikok — sea turtle nests occasionally found; walk carefully.
safety_notes: Swimming not recommended; experienced surfers only in surf zone.

characteristics.depth_profile: Shallow windy bay popular for kite launch — not for swimming.
characteristics.stone_rocks: Rocky and windy shoreline
characteristics.access: 4x4 recommended
fun_facts: Popular with kitesurfers who want Atlantic-side wind and waves.

characteristics.depth_profile: Beach at base of Aruba's highest sand dunes — water too rough for entry.
characteristics.stone_rocks: Dunes and rocky coast
characteristics.access: 4x4; often on jeep/UTV tours
fun_facts: Spectacular dune landscape above crashing waves — viewpoint more than swim spot.

characteristics.depth_profile: Rocky shore with immediate drop-off near volcanic boulders.
characteristics.stone_rocks: Dominant — smooth black volcanic pebbles and boulders instead of white sand
characteristics.sand: black stone shingle — no classic sand beach
characteristics.access: 4x4 or long walk
fun_facts: Aruba's most visually unusual shore — black stones against turquoise water.

characteristics.depth_profile: Rocky surf coast — strong Atlantic surge.
characteristics.stone_rocks: Extensive limestone and coral rock
characteristics.access: 4x4 north coast
fun_facts: Remote north-coast cove visited by surfers and fishermen; dramatic untamed coastline.

characteristics.depth_profile: Sandy cove with gradual depth on calm days.
characteristics.stone_rocks: Some rock outcrops at edges
characteristics.access: South coast; rental car recommended
fun_facts: Little-known south-coast spot — locals picnic here away from resort crowds.
